requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate thyroid structural disease, compressive features, or thyroid inflammatory/autoimmune pattern. Goiter, tenderness, asymmetry, or a dominant nodule changes etiology and imaging decisions.
requiredExam.rationale: Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).
requiredExam.technique: Inspect and palpate the thyroid from in front or behind while the patient swallows, noting size, tenderness, asymmetry, nodules, fixation, or bruit if auscultated.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: During thyroid hormone or thyroid structural evaluation
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: thyroid structural disease, compressive features, or thyroid inflammatory/autoimmune pattern
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Goiter, tenderness, asymmetry, or a dominant nodule changes etiology and imaging decisions.
requiredExam.limitations: Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.

treatmentOptions.diagnostic_target: Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.: patient data that changes testing, treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).
treatmentOptions.management_change: Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.
treatmentOptions.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
treatmentOptions.limitations: This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).

reference_values: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context.
Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay.
T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific.
Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
decision_steps: Confirm thyrotoxicosis with suppressed TSH plus elevated free T4 and/or T3, then determine etiology with TRAb/TSI, uptake scan, Doppler ultrasound, medication/iodine history, or thyroiditis labs.
Differentiate high-uptake hormone overproduction from low-uptake thyroiditis, exogenous hormone, iodine/amiodarone effect, or destructive thyroiditis because treatment differs.
Assess severity with heart rate/rhythm, fever, heart failure, mental status, liver disease, pregnancy, orbitopathy, and thyroid storm features.
For nodular or uncertain disease, evaluate autonomous nodules and malignancy-risk features before definitive therapy.
Confirm that Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) is the selected clinical intent, then screen for red flags before routine outpatient testing.
Order the first-line tests in sequence: TSH, free T4, total/free T3; TRAb/TSI; RAIU or Doppler ultrasound when etiology unclear.

Interpret numeric results against the workup cutoffs and local assay ranges: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay.

If the initial result is equivocal, discordant with the phenotype, or affected by medication, acute illness, pregnancy, age, sex, or assay interference, repeat or confirm using the next guideline test before committing to long-term therapy.
treatment_options: Use beta-blocker symptom control when not contraindicated for adrenergic symptoms or tachycardia.
Use antithyroid drugs for Graves/toxic nodular hyperthyroidism when appropriate, with pregnancy trimester, liver disease, agranulocytosis counseling, and baseline labs considered.
Choose radioactive iodine, surgery, or continued medical therapy based on etiology, goiter/nodule features, orbitopathy, pregnancy plans, contraindications, and patient preference.
Treat thyroiditis primarily with supportive care, NSAID or glucocorticoid when indicated, and avoid antithyroid drugs when hormone release rather than synthesis is the mechanism.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid); draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
For confirmed Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid), choose therapy based on severity, comorbidities, pregnancy/fertility context, contraindications, patient preference, and local specialty pathway.
Monitor treatment response with the condition-specific clinical endpoint plus the most relevant labs or imaging listed in this workup.
Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.

exact_data_needed: Any palpitations, tremor, heat intolerance, anxiety, insomnia, weight loss, diarrhea, or sweating suggesting thyroid hormone excess or adrenergic physiology?
How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms?
Which medications, supplements, missed doses, biotin, iodine/contrast exposure, hormone therapy, or recent treatment changes could alter Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) interpretation or safety?
Do you have anterior neck pain or thyroid tenderness, painful swallowing, recent viral illness, fever, or postpartum timing suggesting thyroiditis?

Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup

Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay.

Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease.
Suppressed TSH plus high FT4/T3 confirms overt hyperthyroidism.
TRAb or uptake pattern changes etiology-specific treatment.
Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.
Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.

Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay.: 0.4-4.0 mIU/L | TSH, free T4, total/free T3.: 0.4-4.0 mIU/L

hyperthyroidism_treatment_bundle.description: Start Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) treatment or disposition only after the active diagnostic/severity thresholds and treatment-safety data are available.

criteria_options.label: Overt thyrotoxicosis biochemistry: TSH below LLN with free T4 and/or T3 above assay ULN
criteria_options.criteria_text: Classify overt thyrotoxicosis when TSH is below the assay lower limit and free T4 and/or T3 are above the assay upper limit; then determine cause with TRAb, uptake/scan, ultrasound, medication, or thyroiditis context.
data_needed: TRAb/TSI or uptake pattern when needed
criteria_options.source_section: Diagnosis and management of thyrotoxicosis

criteria_options.label: Subclinical hyperthyroidism high-risk stratum: persistent TSH <0.1 mU/L over 3 to 6 months; treat age >65 or cardiac/osteoporosis/symptomatic risk
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA considers TSH <0.1 mU/L on repeated measurement over 3 to 6 months persistent; treatment is recommended/considered based on age >65 years, cardiac disease, osteoporosis, menopausal status, symptoms, and risk.
criteria_options.source_section: Subclinical hyperthyroidism recommendations 73-76

criteria_options.label: Subclinical hyperthyroidism low-detectable stratum: persistent TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; consider treatment age >=65 or cardiac/osteoporosis/symptoms, otherwise monitor 6-12 months
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA describes low but detectable TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; treatment is considered for age >=65 years or risk factors, while asymptomatic age <65 without cardiac disease/osteoporosis can be observed with 6-12 month monitoring.
criteria_options.source_section: Subclinical hyperthyroidism recommendations 75-76 and Table 10

criteria_options.label: Subclinical hyperthyroidism high-risk stratum: persistent TSH <0.1 mU/L over 3 to 6 months; treat age >65 or cardiac/osteoporosis/symptomatic risk
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA considers TSH <0.1 mU/L on repeated measurement over 3 to 6 months persistent; treatment is recommended/considered based on age >65 years, cardiac disease, osteoporosis, menopausal status, symptoms, and risk.
criteria_options.source_section: Subclinical hyperthyroidism recommendations 73-76

criteria_options.label: Subclinical hyperthyroidism low-detectable stratum: persistent TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; consider treatment age >=65 or cardiac/osteoporosis/symptoms, otherwise monitor 6-12 months
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA describes low but detectable TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; treatment is considered for age >=65 years or risk factors, while asymptomatic age <65 without cardiac disease/osteoporosis can be observed with 6-12 month monitoring.

children.edgeLabel: Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) active from documented findings: Any palpitations, tremor, heat intolerance, anxiety, insomnia, weight loss, diarrhea, or sweating suggesting thyroid hormone excess or adrenergic physiology?; How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms?; Which medications, supplements, missed doses, biotin, iodine/contrast exposure, hormone therapy, or recent treatment changes could alter Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) interpretation or safety?; Do you have anterior neck pain or thyroid tenderness, painful swallowing, recent viral illness, fever, or postpartum timing suggesting thyroiditis?; Thyroid storm: fever, severe tachycardia/AF, heart failure, agitation, GI symptoms, altered mental status.; Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.; TSH, free T4, total/free T3.

criteria.description: Obtain Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) diagnostic/severity/treatment-safety data together rather than as a one-step chain.

children.action: Thyroid storm: fever, severe tachycardia/AF, heart failure, agitation, GI symptoms, altered mental status.; Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.; Thyroid storm, myxedema coma, atrial fibrillation/heart failure, vision-threatening orbitopathy, rapidly enlarging neck mass, hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes.; Measure blood pressure. Use beta-blocker symptom control when not contraindicated for adrenergic symptoms or tachycardia.; Use antithyroid drugs for Graves/toxic nodular hyperthyroidism when appropriate, with pregnancy trimester, liver disease, agranulocytosis counseling, and baseline labs considered.; Choose radioactive iodine, surgery, or continued medical therapy based on etiology, goiter/nodule features, orbitopathy, pregnancy plans, contraindications, and patient preference.; Treat thyroiditis primarily with supportive care, NSAID or glucocorticoid when indicated, and avoid antithyroid drugs when hormone release rather than synthesis is the mechanism.; Suppressed TSH plus high FT4/T3 confirms overt hyperthyroidism.; TRAb or uptake pattern changes etiology-specific treatment.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.

children.label: Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid): initiate treatment/disposition for the active threshold-defined branch
children.edgeLabel: Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) treatment/disposition indicated by: TSH below LLN; free T4 above ULN; T3 above ULN; TSH <0.1 mU/L; 3 to 6 months; age >65 years; Use beta-blocker symptom control when not contraindicated for adrenergic symptoms or tachycardia.; Use antithyroid drugs for Graves/toxic nodular hyperthyroidism when appropriate, with pregnancy trimester, liver disease, agranulocytosis counseling, and baseline labs considered.; Choose radioactive iodine, surgery, or continued medical therapy based on etiology, goiter/nodule features, orbitopathy, pregnancy plans, contraindications, and patient preference.; Treat thyroiditis primarily with supportive care, NSAID or glucocorticoid when indicated, and avoid antithyroid drugs when hormone release rather than synthesis is the mechanism.; Suppressed TSH plus high FT4/T3 confirms overt hyperthyroidism.; TRAb or uptake pattern changes etiology-specific treatment.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.

criteria.description: Start Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) treatment or disposition only after the active diagnostic/severity thresholds and treatment-safety data are available.

criteria_options.label: Overt thyrotoxicosis biochemistry: TSH below LLN with free T4 and/or T3 above assay ULN
criteria_options.criteria_text: Classify overt thyrotoxicosis when TSH is below the assay lower limit and free T4 and/or T3 are above the assay upper limit; then determine cause with TRAb, uptake/scan, ultrasound, medication, or thyroiditis context.
data_needed: TRAb/TSI or uptake pattern when needed
criteria_options.source_section: Diagnosis and management of thyrotoxicosis

criteria_options.label: Subclinical hyperthyroidism high-risk stratum: persistent TSH <0.1 mU/L over 3 to 6 months; treat age >65 or cardiac/osteoporosis/symptomatic risk
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA considers TSH <0.1 mU/L on repeated measurement over 3 to 6 months persistent; treatment is recommended/considered based on age >65 years, cardiac disease, osteoporosis, menopausal status, symptoms, and risk.
criteria_options.source_section: Subclinical hyperthyroidism recommendations 73-76

criteria_options.label: Subclinical hyperthyroidism low-detectable stratum: persistent TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; consider treatment age >=65 or cardiac/osteoporosis/symptoms, otherwise monitor 6-12 months
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA describes low but detectable TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; treatment is considered for age >=65 years or risk factors, while asymptomatic age <65 without cardiac disease/osteoporosis can be observed with 6-12 month monitoring.
criteria_options.source_section: Subclinical hyperthyroidism recommendations 75-76 and Table 10

children.label: Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid): escalate when reassessment worsens or contradicts expected course
children.edgeLabel: Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) reassessment worsens or contradicts expected response: TSH below LLN; free T4 above ULN; T3 above ULN; TSH <0.1 mU/L; TSH, free T4, total/free T3.; TRAb/TSI.; RAIU or Doppler ultrasound when etiology unclear.; Measure blood pressure

criteria.description: Escalate Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) if symptoms, vitals, critical thresholds, imaging/ECG/procedure results, or treatment response worsen or no longer fit the selected branch.

criteria_options.label: Overt thyrotoxicosis biochemistry: TSH below LLN with free T4 and/or T3 above assay ULN
criteria_options.criteria_text: Classify overt thyrotoxicosis when TSH is below the assay lower limit and free T4 and/or T3 are above the assay upper limit; then determine cause with TRAb, uptake/scan, ultrasound, medication, or thyroiditis context.
data_needed: TRAb/TSI or uptake pattern when needed
criteria_options.source_section: Diagnosis and management of thyrotoxicosis

criteria.description: Stop, narrow, taper, or de-escalate Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) treatment only when objective response, diagnosis certainty, and cited stopping criteria support it.

criteria_options.label: Subclinical hyperthyroidism high-risk stratum: persistent TSH <0.1 mU/L over 3 to 6 months; treat age >65 or cardiac/osteoporosis/symptomatic risk
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA considers TSH <0.1 mU/L on repeated measurement over 3 to 6 months persistent; treatment is recommended/considered based on age >65 years, cardiac disease, osteoporosis, menopausal status, symptoms, and risk.
criteria_options.source_section: Subclinical hyperthyroidism recommendations 73-76

criteria_options.label: Subclinical hyperthyroidism low-detectable stratum: persistent TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; consider treatment age >=65 or cardiac/osteoporosis/symptoms, otherwise monitor 6-12 months
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA describes low but detectable TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; treatment is considered for age >=65 years or risk factors, while asymptomatic age <65 without cardiac disease/osteoporosis can be observed with 6-12 month monitoring.

parallel_actions: Use beta-blocker symptom control when not contraindicated for adrenergic symptoms or tachycardia.
Use antithyroid drugs for Graves/toxic nodular hyperthyroidism when appropriate, with pregnancy trimester, liver disease, agranulocytosis counseling, and baseline labs considered.
Choose radioactive iodine, surgery, or continued medical therapy based on etiology, goiter/nodule features, orbitopathy, pregnancy plans, contraindications, and patient preference.
Treat thyroiditis primarily with supportive care, NSAID or glucocorticoid when indicated, and avoid antithyroid drugs when hormone release rather than synthesis is the mechanism.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid); draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
Suppressed TSH plus high FT4/T3 confirms overt hyperthyroidism.
TRAb or uptake pattern changes etiology-specific treatment.
Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Overt thyrotoxicosis biochemistry: TSH below LLN with free T4 and/or T3 above assay ULN
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Classify overt thyrotoxicosis when TSH is below the assay lower limit and free T4 and/or T3 are above the assay upper limit; then determine cause with TRAb, uptake/scan, ultrasound, medication, or thyroiditis context.
data_needed: TRAb/TSI or uptake pattern when needed
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Diagnosis and management of thyrotoxicosis

guideline_cutoffs.label: Subclinical hyperthyroidism high-risk stratum: persistent TSH <0.1 mU/L over 3 to 6 months; treat age >65 or cardiac/osteoporosis/symptomatic risk
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA considers TSH <0.1 mU/L on repeated measurement over 3 to 6 months persistent; treatment is recommended/considered based on age >65 years, cardiac disease, osteoporosis, menopausal status, symptoms, and risk.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Subclinical hyperthyroidism recommendations 73-76

guideline_cutoffs.label: Subclinical hyperthyroidism low-detectable stratum: persistent TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; consider treatment age >=65 or cardiac/osteoporosis/symptoms, otherwise monitor 6-12 months
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA describes low but detectable TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; treatment is considered for age >=65 years or risk factors, while asymptomatic age <65 without cardiac disease/osteoporosis can be observed with 6-12 month monitoring.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Subclinical hyperthyroidism recommendations 75-76 and Table 10

Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay.; Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease.

criteria.description: Use an alternate pathway when Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) criteria are absent, a cited mimic better explains the presentation, or objective result data contradict this workup.

criteria_options.source_section: Diagnosis and management of thyrotoxicosis

criteria_options.label: Subclinical hyperthyroidism high-risk stratum: persistent TSH <0.1 mU/L over 3 to 6 months; treat age >65 or cardiac/osteoporosis/symptomatic risk
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA considers TSH <0.1 mU/L on repeated measurement over 3 to 6 months persistent; treatment is recommended/considered based on age >65 years, cardiac disease, osteoporosis, menopausal status, symptoms, and risk.
criteria_options.source_section: Subclinical hyperthyroidism recommendations 73-76

criteria_options.label: Subclinical hyperthyroidism low-detectable stratum: persistent TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; consider treatment age >=65 or cardiac/osteoporosis/symptoms, otherwise monitor 6-12 months
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA describes low but detectable TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; treatment is considered for age >=65 years or risk factors, while asymptomatic age <65 without cardiac disease/osteoporosis can be observed with 6-12 month monitoring.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Overt thyrotoxicosis biochemistry: TSH below LLN with free T4 and/or T3 above assay ULN
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Classify overt thyrotoxicosis when TSH is below the assay lower limit and free T4 and/or T3 are above the assay upper limit; then determine cause with TRAb, uptake/scan, ultrasound, medication, or thyroiditis context.
data_needed: TRAb/TSI or uptake pattern when needed

guideline_cutoffs.label: Subclinical hyperthyroidism high-risk stratum: persistent TSH <0.1 mU/L over 3 to 6 months; treat age >65 or cardiac/osteoporosis/symptomatic risk
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA considers TSH <0.1 mU/L on repeated measurement over 3 to 6 months persistent; treatment is recommended/considered based on age >65 years, cardiac disease, osteoporosis, menopausal status, symptoms, and risk.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Subclinical hyperthyroidism recommendations 73-76

guideline_cutoffs.label: Subclinical hyperthyroidism low-detectable stratum: persistent TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; consider treatment age >=65 or cardiac/osteoporosis/symptoms, otherwise monitor 6-12 months
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA describes low but detectable TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; treatment is considered for age >=65 years or risk factors, while asymptomatic age <65 without cardiac disease/osteoporosis can be observed with 6-12 month monitoring.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Subclinical hyperthyroidism recommendations 75-76 and Table 10

Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) danger feature or high-risk cutoff: Thyroid storm: fever, severe tachycardia/AF, heart failure, agitation, GI symptoms, altered mental status.; Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.; Thyroid storm, myxedema coma, atrial fibrillation/heart failure, vision-threatening orbitopathy, rapidly enlarging neck mass, hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes.; Measure blood pressure; TSH below LLN; free T4 above ULN; T3 above ULN; Suppressed TSH plus high FT4/T3 confirms overt hyperthyroidism.

Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) mimic/exclusion better fits: Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup; Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay.; Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease.

Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) modifier changes treatment/disposition: Measure blood pressure; Measure heart rate; Document mental status; Measure temperature; pregnancy/postpartum/lactation status; renal/hepatic/cardiac disease; allergy or drug interaction; pediatric/adult applicability

clinical_cutoff_criteria.criteria_text: ATA considers TSH <0.1 mU/L on repeated measurement over 3 to 6 months persistent; treatment is recommended/considered based on age >65 years, cardiac disease, osteoporosis, menopausal status, symptoms, and risk.

clinical_cutoff_criteria.label: Subclinical hyperthyroidism low-detectable stratum: persistent TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; consider treatment age >=65 or cardiac/osteoporosis/symptoms, otherwise monitor 6-12 months
clinical_cutoff_criteria.criteria_text: ATA describes low but detectable TSH 0.1-0.4 mU/L; treatment is considered for age >=65 years or risk factors, while asymptomatic age <65 without cardiac disease/osteoporosis can be observed with 6-12 month monitoring.

clinical_cutoff_review.review_note: Cutoff rows were added because the prior module evidence did not expose enough explicit guideline thresholds for pathway traversal.
